answer: The canonical way to share information across modules within a single program is to create a special module (often called config or cfg). Just import the config module in all modules of your application; the module then becomes available as a global name. Because there is only one instance of each module, any changes made to the module object get reflected everywhere.
Default values are created exactly once, when the function is defined, and changes to mutable objects can lead to confusion, as the same object is reused across function calls.
The slash (`/`) in a Python function's argument list denotes that the parameters prior to it are positional-only, meaning they can only be specified positionally and not by keyword.
